gotest.details: 38 tests pass with -race flag. Run twice with identical results (5.562s first, 5.856s second). No flaky tests detected. Packages: integration_test.go contains all 38 tests; sub-packages have no test files.

curl.details: curl 8.6.0 installed at /usr/bin/curl. Successfully connected to yaad REST API at http://localhost:3456/yaad/health — returned {"status":"ok","version":"0.1.0"}. Server already listening on :3456 (REST) and :3457 (gRPC).

grpcurl.details: grpcurl is NOT installed. gRPC server is listening on :3457 with methods: Remember, Recall, Context, Forget, Link, Unlink, Subgraph, Impact, SessionStart, SessionEnd, Health, GraphStats (unary), and WatchMemories (stream). To test gRPC endpoints, install grpcurl: 'brew install grpcurl' or 'go install github.com/fullstorydev/grpcurl/cmd/grpcurl@latest'. Alternatively, gRPC can be tested programmatically via Go client code.

binary.details: Binary built successfully: go build -o /tmp/yaad-test-bin ./cmd/yaad (avoided directory conflict with /tmp/yaad-test). --help output shows 38 available commands. 'yaad status' reports 0 nodes, 0 edges, 0 sessions across default .yaad/ database.

tuistory.details: tuistory is not a standalone binary — it is a Factory skill available via the Skill tool. TUI testing can be performed by invoking the 'tuistory' skill which automates terminal UI interactions. The yaad binary has a 'tui' subcommand for interactive terminal UI that can be tested this way.

resources.rationale: System has 8GB RAM and 8 CPU cores. Baseline free+inactive memory is ~2066 MB. Tests consume ~289 MB max RSS with race detection (~5.8s) and ~289 MB without race (~2.15s wall time). Memory is not a bottleneck — at 289MB per test run with 2066MB baseline, you could fit 7 concurrent runs before memory pressure. However, CPU cores are the limiting factor: with 8 cores and ~2s wall time per test run (non-race), running 5 concurrent validators leaves 3 cores for OS and other processes. Recommending 5 as a safe max to avoid CPU contention while maximizing throughput.
